title: taraxacum cantabricum
intro: Taraxacum cantabricum, also known as Cantabrian dandelion, is a plant species of the dandelion genus that is native to the Cantabrian Mountains in Spain. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. Taraxacum cantabricum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be extracted and utilized in a variety of forms.
